Document:
# Protogens
[color=#ffa500]Warning! This species is not recommended for new players due to major gameplay changes![/color]
Protogens are not technically a species, but rather a broad term for a person that has modified themselves extensively with cybernetic enhancements that they can no longer be considered their own species anymore.
Despite "Cyborg" already existing as a term, it was coined for machines that had organic brains, and as such a distinction needed to be made.
## Racial Features
- Receive [color=#ffa500]more heat damage[/color], [color=#8147ff]more shock damage[/color], [color=#FF3636]more blunt damage[/color] and [color=#1e90ff]less cold damage[/color], depending on your subspecies. - Variable [color=#B9BF93]resistances[/color] depending on your cybernetic frame. (Access your species specific loadout from the Appearance tab) - Healing sources are [color=red]50% less effective[/color] on you, but you can benefit from both chemical and mechanical repairs. - You can heal [color=#64ffe6]poison damage[/color] and [color=#C8FF75]genetic damage[/color] with Uranium Glass and Reinforced Uranium Glass. - EMPs are [color=red]dangerous[/color] to your cybernetics! Don't let yourself get hit, or you'll be immobilised for quite a while. - Subspeces selections - You can choose between being an artifically made protogen with no original species, or Proto-(Species). They inherit their original species traits and have modified resistances, combining them together into something new. - You can modify your Protogen frames and lights in Markings.
